citations.claim: The coordinator is the central routing and trust layer in the Darkbloom network that accepts provider WebSocket connections, verifies Secure Enclave attestations, and routes OpenAI-compatible requests
The coordinator runs in a GCP Confidential VM (AMD SEV-SNP) with hardware-encrypted memory and reads requests for routing but never logs prompt content
Server is the main HTTP/WS server that ties together the provider registry, key store, payment ledger, billing service, and HTTP routing with various configuration options
The registry manages the set of connected provider agents, their capabilities, and routes inference requests using round-robin among idle providers with trust level filtering
Store provides storage backends with MemoryStore for development and PostgresStore for production, managing double-entry ledger in micro-USD
Attestation verifier checks signed attestation blobs using P-256 ECDSA keys from Apple Secure Enclave, validating hardware identity and security state
Billing service provides unified payment processing with Stripe Checkout for deposits and Stripe Connect Express for provider payouts
Protocol defines WebSocket message types for coordinator-provider communication including registration, heartbeats, inference requests with optional X25519/NaCl-box encryption
Consumer handlers implement OpenAI-compatible endpoints with sender-sealed requests, trusted routing in Confidential VM, and re-encryption to providers
Payment ledger tracks consumer balances and provider payouts with all amounts in micro-USD, supporting Stripe payments and settlements
PostgreSQL connectivity via jackc/pgx/v5 driver
WebSocket communication via nhooyr.io/websocket library
Datadog integration for monitoring via datadog-go and dd-trace-go libraries, JWT authentication, UUID generation, and crypto libraries
API surface includes OpenAI-compatible consumer endpoints, authentication/billing endpoints, provider WebSocket, admin operations, and public stats
Inference request flow includes balance reservation, model validation, provider routing with retry logic, and streaming response handling
Data persistence supports PostgreSQL for production and in-memory store for development with explicit opt-in via EIGENINFERENCE_ALLOW_MEMORY_STORE
Stripe billing integration with webhook secrets, success/cancel URLs, and Connect Express configuration for provider payouts
Datadog APM tracer integration with service/environment configuration and DogStatsD metrics client
MicroMDM integration for independent provider security verification with callback handling for MDA certificate storage
Trust levels include none (no attestation), self_signed (provider's own key), and hardware (MDM + MDA + SE key bound to Apple-verified hardware)
Inference timeout is 600 seconds (10 minutes) to allow 32k tokens at ~55 tok/s on slower hardware, with retry logic up to 3 attempts
